text: Literally, in the Greek text, ‘have sat on the ground’. The Latin version, perhaps through an error, reads ‘have sat on their thrones’.

v. 31-36: The exact bearing of these verses cannot be determined. In verse 32, ‘unquiet hearts’ is literally ‘proud hearts’; but pride seems irrelevant to the present context, and it is likely that the original Hebrew text had ‘hearts of aliens’ (as in verse 36). In that case the whole passage may be a warning against undue fraternization with Gentiles.
